sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Fund Alchemy, a Cardano-native Bitcoin treasury protocol and BTCfi infrastructure layer with reserve architecture, FIRE and ICE assets, dashboards, integration adapters, monthly reporting, audits, legal/compliance work, go-to-market execution, and staged launch liquidity.
Recipient: Sundial Protocol and Charms, with Intersect proposed as interim fund administrator subject to confirmation and final agreement.

Deliverables: Shared BTC reserve architecture with transparent reserve-ratio mechanics and safety zones, Composable Cardano-native FIRE and ICE assets, Open-source SDKs and adapters for wallets, DEXs, and DeFi protocols, Always-on dashboards for reserve ratio, supply, liquidity health, fee flows, and deployment status, Monthly governance reports and BTCfi reporting standards, Independent security review, audit, and economic modeling, Three-month staged launch liquidity deployment, Legal, compliance, disclosures, treasury reporting, and launch-readiness review, Ecosystem education, DRep communications, partner activation, and launch coordination
Deadline/expiry: Three-month staged liquidity deployment after enactment with monthly reporting; administrator confirmation expected within 30 days of enactment.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 10,000,000 ada, using an approximate USD 0.20 per ada reference rate for an approximately USD 2.0 million planning budget split into two roughly USD 1.0 million pools.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Pool 1 is intended for protocol infrastructure and staged treasury-supported launch liquidity, deployed in three monthly tranches of approximately USD 250,000, USD 250,000, and USD 500,000 after specified reviews and performance checks.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Pool 2 is intended for delivery, audit, integrations, dashboards, governance reporting, legal/compliance work, administration, and go-to-market execution with itemized planning allocations.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Alchemy will create a shared BTC reserve architecture for FIRE and ICE using a reserve-ratio formula, a 5.0x initial target reserve ratio, and safety zones above 4.0x, between 2.0x and 4.0x, and below 2.0x.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): FIRE is described as a volatility-absorbing residual BTC-upside asset, while ICE is described as a lower-volatility USD-denominated BTC-backed asset, both intended to be composable Cardano-native primitives.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal states that profits, yield, and returns from treasury-supported launch liquidity will be returned to the Cardano Treasury quarterly, while principal may be returned after Alchemy reaches a 30-day time-weighted average TVL of at least USD 60 million and governance approves a return action.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal asserts that Cardano currently lacks a production BTCfi infrastructure layer and risks Bitcoin-backed structured exposure consolidating on Ethereum, BNB, Solana, and off-chain markets if Alchemy is not funded.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 10,000,000 ada, using an approximate USD 0.20 per ada reference rate for an approximately USD 2.0 million planning budget split into two roughly USD 1.0 million pools." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal asserts that Cardano currently lacks a production BTCfi infrastructure layer and risks Bitcoin-backed structured exposure consolidating on Ethereum, BNB, Solana, and off-chain markets if Alchemy is not funded." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
